title: ctenitis pseudorhodolepis
intro: Ctenitis pseudorhodolepis, also known as the False Hemitomes fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the subtropical regions of China and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Ctenitis pseudorhodolepis are primarily attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
